Here is a sample PlantUML diagram generated using the following description:

```
+---------------+
|  Plant UML Diagram |
+---------------+
           |
                  |
                  v
          +---------------+
           |  Node 1     |
           |  (Source node)    |
           +---------------+
                  |
                  |
                  v
          +---------------+
           |  Node 2     |
           |  (Response node) |
           +---------------+
```

To generate a sample PlantUML diagram, we can follow these steps:

1. Define the plant structure (see PlantUML diagram example below): For each of the following components, generate a sample diagram with syntax errors or correct them:
 	* `@startuml`: Add @startuml tags to the source code for Node 1 (Source node)
2. Generate the sample diagrams using the `@enduml` tag format: For each of the plant structures (Node 1, Node 2, etc.), generate the corresponding diagram by replacing the @startuml tags with the correct ones.
3. Remove duplicates to avoid redundant code generation and maintain consistency in notation
4. Generate the diagrams for all the other components using the `@enduml` tag format: For all the other plant structures (Node 2, Node 3, etc.), generate the corresponding diagrams by replacing the @enduml tags with the correct ones.
